mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities by lowering discount rates and improving risk appetite, so monthly rate cuts predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS in any 3-year rolling window, or if the triggered sign is negative more often than positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days as investors price weaker earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if, after UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points in 3 months, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases or the average return is not below the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates broad final demand strength that supports sales and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and GDPC1 do not outperform either series alone in explaining 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint-signal hit rate is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and inventory demand, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after INDPRO crosses below its 3-month average, or if the effect disappears in more than half of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below recent levels signals housing demand contraction, weaker wealth effects, and slower credit-sensitive spending, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still low, real policy stays accommodative and equities should rise over the next 60 trading days as liquidity supports valuation multiples.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with inflation above FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points do not deliver positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses when inflation is high and rates are unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and decline in PAYEMS identifies labor-market deterioration that directly weakens consumption expectations and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined labor deterioration does not outperform either standalone series in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the triggered hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A broad monthly slowdown in RSAFS, HOUST, and GDPC1 indicates demand retrenchment that should depress equities over the next 60 trading days as earnings and revenue expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: GDP, retail sales, and housing all decline versus their prior readings
failure_condition: Reject if the three-way demand contraction does not produce lower-than-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if any one series alone explains the effect as well as the joint signal.
